channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Michigan muscles its way to program's 2nd NCAA basketball title, beating UConn
item.description: High-scoring Michigan had to get down and dirty to dig out the national title Monday, making only two 3-pointers all night but still muscling its way to a 69-63 victory over stingy, stubborn UConn.
item.content:encoded:
High-scoring Michigan had to get down and dirty to dig out the national title Monday, making only two 3-pointers all night but still muscling its way to a 69-63 victory over stingy, stubborn UConn.

(Image credit: Michael Conroy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How a free pop-up health clinic in Texas is reaching a community of fishermen
item.description: Amid a rise in healthcare costs and declining federal support for commercial fishermen, a free pop-up health clinic in Galveston, Texas is reaching a community of Vietnamese fishermen.
item.content:encoded: Amid a rise in healthcare costs and declining federal support for commercial fishermen, a free pop-up health clinic in Galveston, Texas is reaching a community of Vietnamese fishermen.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Myong

item.title: Supreme Court clears the way for Bannon contempt case to be dismissed
item.description: Bannon spent four months in prison after defying a subpoena from the House Select Committee investigating the Jan. 6 attack.
item.content:encoded:
Bannon spent four months in prison after defying a subpoena from the House Select Committee investigating the Jan. 6 attack.

(Image credit: Kayla Bartkowski)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Iran rejects a U.S. ceasefire plan as Trump again threatens to bomb its infrastructure
item.description: Iran's top officials pushed back against a U.S. ceasefire plan and President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.
item.content:encoded:
Iran's top officials pushed back against a U.S. ceasefire plan and President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: These blind students say their college blocked their education. A new rule could help
item.description: Higher education is especially reliant on computers and phones, but accessibility for people with disabilities has often been forgotten. A new federal rule could change that.
item.content:encoded:
Higher education is especially reliant on computers and phones, but accessibility for people with disabilities has often been forgotten. A new federal rule could change that.

(Image credit: Kristian Thacker for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: Savannah Guthrie returns to the 'Today' show months after her mother's disappearance
item.description: Savannah Guthrie's mother, Nancy Guthrie, has not been seen since returning home from a family dinner the evening of Jan. 31.
item.content:encoded: Savannah Guthrie's mother, Nancy Guthrie, has not been seen since returning home from a family dinner the evening of Jan. 31.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Artemis II astronauts heading home after historic moon flyby
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts are on a course back to Earth after flying by the moon Monday, witnessing a solar eclipse, and breaking a distance record set by NASA's Apollo 13 astronauts.
item.content:encoded:
The Artemis II astronauts are on a course back to Earth after flying by the moon Monday, witnessing a solar eclipse, and breaking a distance record set by NASA's Apollo 13 astronauts.

(Image credit: NASA via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: UCLA storms past South Carolina to claim its 1st NCAA women's basketball title
item.description: UCLA secured the first NCAA women's basketball national championship in school history — a goal that was set after losing in the first Final Four last season.
item.content:encoded:
UCLA secured the first NCAA women's basketball national championship in school history — a goal that was set after losing in the first Final Four last season.

(Image credit: Rick Scuteri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: More teens are getting hooked on gambling. Parents say it often goes undetected
item.description: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.content:encoded: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Judge halts Trump effort requiring colleges to show they don't consider race in admissions
item.description: A federal judge on Saturday said the Trump Administration the demand to collect data from universities was rolled out in a "rushed and chaotic" manner.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge on Saturday said the Trump Administration the demand to collect data from universities was rolled out in a "rushed and chaotic" manner.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
